description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a polished, striped wood grain finish. The front face displays a convex, rounded rectangular glass screen bordered by a metallic trim, with two large, cream-colored rotary knobs symmetrically placed at the bottom corners. The brand name 'Iskra' is visible in a metallic script below the screen. The left side of the cabinet has a vertical vent with cream-colored slats, while the back panel is made of perforated dark metal with a protruding, angular housing for the cathode ray tube and various access ports. The right side is plain wood, matching the rest of the cabinet. The television stands on four small, black feet, elevating it slightly above the ground.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, metallic trim, and two large rotary knobs, featuring a striped wood grain cabinet and vented side panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, metallic trim, two rotary knobs, and the 'Iskra' brand logo.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the vertical cream-colored vent and the striped wood grain finish.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the perforated metal back panel, protruding cathode ray tube housing, and access ports.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a plain, continuous wood grain surface with no additional features.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, metallic gold bezel with rounded corners and a glossy finish
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary knobs on the front bottom corners

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
